title: Mail Organizer Cabinet
summary: A tall entryway cabinet combining a hinged waste bin door with a built-in paper shredder cutout, plus three drawers for pending mail, files, and larger storage — a genuine command-center piece.
description:
This cabinet packs several real household functions into one narrow footprint: a hinged door bay sized for a small wastebasket with a built-in paper shredder shelf, and three stacked drawers of increasing size for pencils, files, and general storage. The shredder cutout is the one genuinely fussy detail — trace the shredder's own base as a template, start the jigsaw cut with a drilled starter hole, and fit the shredder in the hole before final trimming rather than cutting to a calculated dimension.

Build the carcass first with the center divider set at the correct distance from the top, using the divider itself as a spacer to nail down the bottom shelf's position accurately — a shortcut that avoids re-measuring the same distance twice. The door goes together INSIDE the face frame opening for a reason: building it in place with even spacing on both sides guarantees a square door with consistent reveals, rather than building a door to a calculated size and hoping it fits.

**Drawer sizing here is genuinely iterative, not calculated once**: build and fit the first drawer, confirm it works with your actual slides and opening, and only then cut the remaining drawers to match. Drawer slide tabs can be bent slightly to adjust fit after the fact — useful, but the plan notes to keep screws inside the tabs so that adjustment stays possible. The false drawer face over the shredder bay gets fitted and finished exactly like the working drawer faces, so the whole front reads as a consistent bank of drawers even though one hides a functional door instead.

materials.name: Wastebasket, under 17 in wide, 11 in deep, 24 in tall
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Shredder base outline traced and cut with a jigsaw.

steps.title: Build the carcass
steps.body: Cut the 1x12s for the carcass. Drill three 3/4" pocket holes on each end of the bottom shelf and attach 31-1/4" down from the top of the sides, using the divider piece as a spacer for consistent placement.

steps.title: Cut the shredder cutout
steps.body: Trace the paper shredder's base as close to the front of its shelf board as possible, centered widthwise. Drill a starter hole and cut out with a jigsaw. Test-fit the shredder and trim as needed.

steps.title: Build the face frame
steps.body: Cut the face frame pieces, lay out on a flat surface, and drill two 3/4" pocket holes per joint. Clamp and attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue. Double-check the shredder side placement before final assembly.

steps.title: Build the door in place
steps.body: Build the door inside the face frame opening for even spacing, attaching the frame pieces to the back with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Add the top
steps.body: Glue and attach the fixed 1x6 top piece to the back of the carcass, then attach the front fixed 1x8 top piece. Attach the final hinged top piece with hinges.

steps.title: Build and fit the drawers
steps.body: Add 1x3 spacers inside the drawer side of the cabinet to space the slides inside the face frame. Build the pencil drawer first, test the fit, then build the remaining drawers to match. Install drawer slides, following the cabinet-member placement against your drawer position.

steps.title: Add drawer and door faces
steps.body: Fit the drawer faces with an even gap around all edges and attach with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, then finish attaching from inside with 1-1/4" screws. Install the door with hinges and a magnetic catch. Fit the false drawer face over the door bay.

steps.title: Add base trim and finish
steps.body: Miter the base moulding at 45° and attach with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. Sand and finish the entire cabinet. Place the wastebasket and shredder inside.
materials: Wastebasket, under 17 in wide, 11 in deep, 24 in tall
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Tall mail organizer cabinet with a shredder bay and three drawers
